data.title: Behind the Scenes of Albemarle's Latest Options Trends
data.description:
Deep-pocketed investors have adopted a bullish approach towards Albemarle (NYSE:ALB), and it's something market players shouldn't ignore. Our tracking of public options records at Benzinga unveiled this significant move today. The identity of these investors remains unknown, but such a substantial move in ALB usually suggests something big is about to happen.

We gleaned this information from our observations today when Benzinga's options scanner highlighted 28 extraordinary options activities for Albemarle. This level of activity is out of the ordinary.

The general mood among these heavyweight investors is divided, with 46% leaning bullish and 39% bearish. Among these notable options, 3 are puts, totaling $118,860, and 25 are calls, amounting to $1,778,274.

What's The Price Target?

After evaluating the trading volumes and Open Interest, it's evident that the major market movers are focusing on a price band between $75.0 and $280.0 for Albemarle, spanning the last three months.

About JD.com

The company has built its own nationwide fulfilment infrastructure and last-mile delivery network, staffed by its own employees, which supports both its online direct sales, its online marketplace and omnichannel businesses.

data.title: Zillow Stock Extends Slide After Earnings As Legal Costs Cloud Outlook
data.description:
Zillow Group Inc (NASDAQ:Z) (NASDAQ:ZG) stock is trading lower Thursday morning, extending Wednesday’s steep sell-off after the online real estate company's latest earnings and guidance. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Zillow stock is showing downward bias. Where is ZG stock headed?

Post-Earnings Volatility Continues Thursday

Thursday’s volatility follows Wednesday's drop, when shares fell more than 17% and hit a new 52-week low after the company reported fourth-quarter results Tuesday evening.

Zillow posted revenue of $654 million for the quarter, topping Wall Street estimates of about $650 million, while adjusted earnings of 39 cents per share came in just shy of the 40 cent consensus.

Management guided first-quarter revenue to a range of $700 million to $710 million, ahead of expectations near $690 million, signaling continued top-line momentum even as investors focus on profitability.

Legal Costs And New Rivals Pressure Valuation

These networks remain a small slice of listings today, but Zillow recently won a preliminary injunction in a dispute tied to Compass Inc and its marketing strategy, a fight that could intensify following Compass's purchase of Anywhere Real Estate Inc. Analysts caution that the legal overhang and structural uncertainty could cap the stock's valuation near term.

Long-Term Upside Hinges On Housing Recovery

Looking further out, McTernan models roughly 50% incremental margins in 2025 excluding legal costs, with potential for incremental adjusted EBITDA margins to reach about 60% in 2027 if legal spending normalizes and the housing market returns to mid-cycle conditions.

That leaves Zillow positioned as a possible beneficiary of a gradual housing recovery, but for now, investors appear focused on the legal bill and competitive risks rather than the long-term upside.

RSI Signals Oversold Conditions

The chart shows Zillow shares moving from mostly neutral levels into oversold territory, with the relative strength index (RSI) recently falling below 30. That drop indicates intensified selling pressure in the wake of recent losses, placing the stock at levels typically associated with stretched downside momentum.

Zillow’s Analyst Outlook

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $80.82. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Lowers Target to $62.00) (Feb. 11)
  • Piper Sandler: Overweight (Lowers Target to $70.00) (Feb. 11)
  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $60.00) (Feb. 11)

Zillow Shares Edge Lower Thursday

ZG Price Action: Zillow Gr shares were down 2.45% at $44.54 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: DraftKings Set To Report Q4 Earnings: What To Watch
data.description:
DraftKings Inc. (NASDAQ:DKNG) shares are in the spotlight Thursday as the company prepares to release fourth-quarter earnings after the market closes.

  • DraftKings stock is showing downward pressure. Where is DKNG stock headed?

What To Know Before DraftKings Reports

DraftKings is expected to report earnings per share of 14 cents on revenue of $1.98 billion. The company has topped earnings per share estimates in three of the last four quarters and beaten revenue estimates once during that period.

In its most recent quarter, DraftKings posted a loss of 26 cents per share, beating the consensus estimate for a 42-cent loss, while revenue of $1.14 billion missed estimates of $1.22 billion.

Investors should keep an eye on user engagement metrics, particularly daily active users (DAU), which are critical for assessing the effectiveness of DraftKings’ marketing strategies. Additionally, watch for trends in average revenue per user (ARPU), as improvements here could signal enhanced monetization of its expanding platform offerings.

“This is the most bullish I have ever felt about our future,” said Jason Robins, DraftKings’ CEO and Co-founder, in the company’s third-quarter earnings report.

“Underlying growth in the business is accelerating and we are excited to launch DraftKings Predictions in the coming months, which we view as a significant incremental opportunity.”

Technical Indicators Reflect Ongoing Bearish Pressure

The stock is currently trading 10.6% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 20.4% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a challenging short-term outlook. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 41.90%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, suggesting ongoing bearish sentiment.

The RSI is at 32.03, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. This combination suggests mixed momentum, reflecting the stock’s struggle to gain traction in the current market environment.

  • Key Resistance: $29.50
  • Key Support: $25.00

DraftKings Stock Remains Flat

DKNG Price Action: At the time of writing, DraftKing stock is trading 1.56% lower at $25.89, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: McDonald's Stock Hits New All-Time Highs And Has More Upside: Analyst Says 'Value Strategy Is Clearly Working'
data.description:
The McDonald's Analysts

  • TD Cowen analyst Andrew Charles maintained a Hold rating on McDonald's stock with a price target of $320.
  • BTIG analyst Peter Saleh reiterated a Buy rating and raised the price target from $360 to $370.

TD Cowen: McDonald's fourth quarter results represent a "strong end to 2025," Charles said in a new investor note.

The analyst cautions that higher interest, higher G&A guidance, and higher-than-expected CapEx guidance could be negatives from the quarterly results.

"We are encouraged by better than expected SSS across all 3 segments, led by both IOM & U.S. strength," Charles said.

The analyst said McDonald's should see around 3% U.S. same-store sales growth in 2026, but the results could be more weighted to strength in the first half of the fiscal year.

Charles was also disappointed with Q4 results that didn't include a launch date for the U.S. specialty beverage refresher. The analyst expects the launch to happen in April.

"Looking ahead, management pledged to continue predictable value in what is anticipated to be a challenged low-income consumer backdrop."

BTIG: The fast food company’s U.S. traffic is up thanks to value promotions, Saleh said in a new investor note.

"I'm lovin' it," Saleh said of McDonald's Q4 results.

The analyst said McDonald's fourth quarter was its strongest quarter in two years, led by double-digit earnings growth and mid-single digit global same-store sales, along with positive traffic trends in the United States.

"The value strategy is clearly working, as the brand recaptures lost share with lower-income guests and widens its lead over competitors."

Saleh said McDonald's menu innovation could enhance its value proposition and drive additional earnings-per-share growth. The analyst also highlighted McDonald's new unit growth guidance, saying the large number was a bit of a surprise.

"We view McDonald's as one of the strongest restaurant concepts in the world that is now in the early innings of the lower-income consumer recovery."

Price Action

McDonald's stock is up 2% to $329.66 on Thursday. Shares hit a new all-time high of $333.38 earlier in Thursday's trading session. The stock price is up 8.7% year-to-date in 2026.

Image: Shutterstock
